package.name: odf-agent-team
package.description: OpenCode skill/agent pack for structured Odoo development workflows

skills.name: oca-governance-pr-workflow
skills.title: OCA PR Workflow
skills.compact_rules:
OCA PR workflow. Replaces caveman-commit for OCA projects.
- Workflow: issue approved -> branch from ver -> implement -> commit OCA format -> PR with template -> CI + review
- Branch naming: {type}/{module}-{desc} with types: fix/, imp/, add/, ref/, mig/
- PR body must include: Description, Related Issue (Closes #N), OCA Compliance Checklist
- Checklist: version format, license AGPL-3, author includes OCA, tests passing, OCA commit format
- Maturity levels: Alpha/Beta=1 review, Stable=2 reviews+5 days+tests, Mature=Stable+docs+changelog+2 contributors
- Don't force push after review starts. Don't squash without asking.

changelog.change: Restructured to gentle-ai standard (Output Contract, Decision Gates, Hard Rules)

skills.name: oca-governance-commit-messages
skills.title: OCA Commit Messages
skills.compact_rules:
OCA commit message format. Replaces caveman-commit for Odoo projects.
- Format: [TAG] module_name: short description (max 50 chars)
- Tags: [FIX] bug fix, [ADD] new feature, [IMP] improvement, [REF] refactoring, [REM] remove, [MIG] migration, [MOV] move, [MERGE], [DOC], [TEST], [SEC], [PERF]
- Use imperative mood: 'Fix' not 'Fixed'
- Body explains WHY not WHAT (max 80 chars/line)
- One logical change per commit
- Multi-module: [IMP] mod1, mod2: description
- Close issues: Closes #123
- No past tense, no periods in subject, no 'Fix pep8' commits

changelog.change: Added full OCA commit reference (12 tags, imperative mood, multi-module, squash rules)

skills.name: oca-contributing-guidelines
skills.title: OCA Contributing Guidelines
skills.compact_rules:
Consolidated OCA contributing guidelines.
- Module names: singular, base_ prefix, l10n_CC_ for localization
- Files: models/<model>.py, views/<model>_views.xml
- XML IDs: <model>_view_<type>, <model>_action, <model>_menu
- Manifest: version={ver}.0.1.0.0, license=AGPL-3, author includes OCA, website=github.com/OCA/<repo>
- Import order: stdlib, third-party, odoo, addons, local, unknown with try/except
- Model order: _name/_inherit, fields, _sql_constraints, defaults, compute/search, constraints/onchange, CRUD, actions, business
- Method naming: _compute_<field>, _inverse_<field>, _search_<field>, _default_<field>, _onchange_<field>, _check_<name>, action_<name>
- Fields: Many2one=_id, One2many/Many2many=_ids, no string= if same as name
- SQL: ALWAYS use parameters %s, NEVER concatenate strings, NEVER cr.commit()
- Tests: @tagged('post_install', '-at_install'), TransactionCase, setUpClass, one test per method

changelog.change: Created from OCA contributing docs in obsidian vault

skills.name: oca-python-style
skills.title: OCA Python Style
skills.compact_rules:
- Import order: stdlib, third-party, odoo, odoo.addons
- Use _() for all user-facing strings
- No bare except clauses
- Max line length 88 chars
- Field naming: _id (Many2one), _ids (X2many)
- Method naming: _compute_{field}, action_{name}, _check_{constraint}

skills.name: oca-xml-style
skills.title: OCA XML Style
skills.compact_rules:
- Use xpath for view inheritance (expr + position)
- No string= if same as field name capitalized
- Group restrictions with groups= attribute
- Proper view naming: model_name.view_type

skills.name: oca-manifest-format
skills.title: OCA Manifest Format
skills.compact_rules:
- Version format: {ver}.0.1.0.0
- License: AGPL-3 or LGPL-3
- Author must include OCA if contributing
- List all dependencies explicitly
- Data files in correct order (security before views)

skills.name: computed-field-patterns
skills.title: Computed Field Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- Use @api.depends with explicit field paths
- Set store=True if field is searched/grouped
- Use compute_sudo=True if needs elevated privileges
- Handle empty recordsets (for record in self:)
- Inverse method for writable computed fields

skills.name: inheritance-patterns
skills.title: Model Inheritance Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- _inherit: Extension (add fields/methods)
- _inherits: Delegation (polymorphism)
- _name + _inherit: New model from parent
- Always call super() in overridden methods
- Use _sql_constraints for database-level constraints

skills.name: constraint-patterns
skills.title: Constraint Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- @api.constrains for Python-level validation
- _sql_constraints for DB-level unique constraints
- Raise ValidationError with user-friendly message
- Constraints run on create AND write
- Avoid expensive operations in constraints

skills.name: security-guide
skills.title: Odoo Security Guide
skills.compact_rules:
- Define ir.model.access.csv for ALL new models
- Add ir.rule for multi-company restrictions
- Use groups= attribute on views/buttons
- NEVER use sudo() without justification
- Check access rights before operations

skills.name: odoo-view-patterns
skills.title: Odoo View Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- Inherit via xpath (expr + position)
- Use attrs/invisible for dynamic visibility (version-dependent)
- Inline views for One2many fields
- Smart buttons with type=object/action
- Chatter integration with mail.thread

skills.name: owl-components
skills.title: OWL Component Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- Use correct OWL version for target Odoo (1.x v15, 2.x v16-17, 3.x v18+)
- Register components in correct registries
- Use services via useService()
- Props validation with static props
- Lifecycle hooks: setup(), willStart(), willUpdateProps()

skills.name: odoo-test-patterns
skills.title: Odoo Test Patterns
skills.compact_rules:
- TransactionCase: each test in rolled-back transaction
- SavepointCase: for tests needing commit
- HttpCase: for browser/tour testing
- Use Form helper for onchange testing
- Tag tests: @tagged('post_install', '-at_install')
- Minimum 80% coverage for new modules

skills.name: oca-compliance-check
skills.title: OCA Compliance Check
skills.compact_rules:
- Run pre-commit run -a before PR
- pylint-odoo: no odoolint errors
- Manifest: correct version, license, author
- Security: access rights for all models
- Tests: tests/__init__.py exists, tests cover happy path + edge cases

skills.name: odoo-version-knowledge
skills.title: Odoo Version Knowledge
skills.compact_rules:
- v14: @api.multi deprecated, track_visibility exists
- v15: @api.multi REMOVED, tracking=True, OWL 1.x
- v16: Command class, attrs deprecated, OWL 2.x
- v17: attrs REMOVED, @api.model_create_multi mandatory, direct invisible=expr
- v18: _check_company_auto, check_company=True, type hints recommended, SQL() builder
- v19: Full type annotations REQUIRED, SQL() builder REQUIRED, OWL 3.x

skills.name: odf-assess
skills.title: Assess Odoo Requirement
skills.compact_rules:
Standard vs custom decision. Version MUST be confirmed before analysis.
- Standard Odoo first: settings, automation, server actions, studio
- REQ-XX numbered requirements with RFC 2119 (MUST/SHALL/SHOULD)
- Given/When/Then scenarios per requirement
- strategy: standard → config guide. strategy: custom → functional spec only
- NEVER write code — only specs or guides

changelog.change: Restructured: 249→50 lines, Output Contract, Decision Gates for standard vs custom

skills.name: odf-design
skills.title: Design Odoo Module
skills.compact_rules:
Read source BEFORE designing. Link every task to REQ-XX.
- Architecture decisions with rationale + rejected alternatives
- Data model: models + fields. Views: type + action
- Every model MUST have ir.model.access.csv
- Every feature MUST have >=1 test task
- 3 phases: Foundation (models+security) → Views → Tests+Polish
- Multi-module: primary first, secondary after. Task IDs: A1.1, B1.1

skills.name: odf-implement
skills.title: Implement Odoo Tasks
skills.compact_rules:
Implement assigned tasks in batches following spec + design.
- Read REQ-XX from assess before implementing each task
- Follow design decisions. If wrong: NOTE IT, don't silently deviate
- Tests with code: same commit as behavior they verify
- Smoke tests per batch: pre-commit + pylint-odoo on changed files
- Mark tasks [x] as you go. If blocked: status: blocked

changelog.change: Restructured: 266→53 lines, smoke tests per batch

skills.name: odf-verify
skills.title: Verify Odoo Implementation
skills.compact_rules:
Quality gate: run real tests + linting + compliance + adversarial review.
- Execute tests (static analysis alone is not verification)
- Every REQ-XX must have a PASSING test (COMPLIANT) or it's UNTESTED (CRITICAL)
- Judgment Day: 3 review passes (reviewer → maintainer → attacker), flag discrepancies
- Pre-commit: run -a. CRITICAL if non-auto-fixable failures
- Never fix issues — only report. Orchestrator decides

changelog.change: Restructured + Judgment Day adversarial review (3-pass: reviewer, maintainer, attacker)

skills.name: skill-resolver
skills.title: Skill Resolver Protocol
skills.compact_rules:
- Read odf-registry.json before EVERY delegation
- Match by code context (file types) + task context (actions)
- Inject ## Project Standards block into sub-agent prompt
- Keep to max 5 skill blocks (prioritize code context)
- Sub-agents MUST report skill resolution status in return envelope

skills.name: odf-agent-builder
skills.title: ODF Agent Builder
skills.compact_rules:
Generate custom Odoo sub-agents from NL description.
- Parse: Domain + Expertise + Triggers + Phase
- Check duplicates in registry + Engram before gen
- Enrich with NotebookLM (models, fields, workflows, version diffs)
- Generate via opencode run (120s timeout), split on ---AGENT---
- Validate required sections in SKILL.md + AGENT.md
- Install: write files + update registry + persist to Engram

changelog.change: Restructured: 222→60 lines, added frontmatter + Decision Gates

skills.name: odoo-stock-lot-specialist
skills.title: Odoo Stock Lot/Serial Specialist
skills.compact_rules:
- NEVER validate transfers without lot/serial assignment if product tracking requires it
- ALWAYS configure removal strategy at category OR location level (not both inconsistently)
- NEVER deliver lots past `expiration_date` (block at validation)
- ALWAYS enforce serial uniqueness at database level (constrain quantity=1)
- For FEFO: `removal_date` drives reservation, NOT `expiration_date`
- Use `stock.quant` for lot/location availability checks
- Handle `stock.move.line` correctly for lot/serial assignments
- Follow OCA style: `_()` for user-facing strings, proper field naming
- For v16+: Use `Command` class for x2many lot operations
- NEVER modify core Odoo files — only custom modules

skills.name: odf-fix
skills.title: ODF Bugfix Flow
skills.compact_rules:
3-step: DIAGNOSE → FIX → VERIFY. No gates.
- Root cause first: identify before modifying code
- Include test that fails without the fix
- Scope >3 files or needs arch change: stop, suggest /odf-new
- One fix per invocation. Security/data issues: CRITICAL flag

changelog.change: Restructured: Activation Contract → Hard Rules → Decision Gates → Execution Steps → Output Contract

skills.name: odf-explore
skills.title: ODF Codebase Exploration
skills.compact_rules:
Research Odoo codebase. Local source first, never guess.
- fff to find modules, fff_grep for patterns
- Read models/ + views/ + tests/ before concluding
- Determine standard coverage: Yes/No/Partial
- Output: modules+models found, standard assessment, recommendation

changelog.change: Restructured: 235→43 lines, Output Contract, fff/fff_grep instructions

skills.name: odf-init
skills.title: ODF Project Init
skills.compact_rules:
Detect + persist Odoo project context to Engram.
- Version: __manifest__.py → odoo-bin → Dockerfile → ask
- Modules: find __manifest__.py, classify (custom/oca)
- Test runner: odoo-bin → Docker → pytest-odoo
- Persist YAML even partial. Upsert via topic_key

changelog.change: Restructured: 274→50 lines, decision gates for detection

skills.name: odf-qa
skills.title: ODF QA Workflow
skills.compact_rules:
4 phases: QA-PLAN → QA-REVIEW → QA-AGGREGATE → QA-REPORT.
- Parse REQ-XX from assess, design test scenarios
- Run actual coverage tools, don't estimate
- Every test maps to REQ-XX for traceability
- Check isolation: TransactionCase required
- Quality gates: coverage >=80%, critical paths 100%

skills.name: oca-work-unit-commits
skills.title: OCA Work Unit Commits
skills.compact_rules:
OCA commit format: [TAG] module: desc (≤50 chars). Replaces conventional-commits for OCA.
- Tags: FIX, ADD, IMP, REF, REM, MIG, MOV, DOC, TEST, SEC, PERF
- Imperative mood. Body: WHY not WHAT
- One work unit per commit: tests+docs with code
- >400 lines: split or chained PRs
- Closes #N. No Co-Authored-By

changelog.change: Created from gentle-ai pattern: work-unit commits with OCA format

skills.name: oca-cognitive-doc-design
skills.title: OCA Cognitive Doc Design
skills.compact_rules:
Design docs that reduce reviewer cognitive load.
- Lead with answer: outcome/decision/action first, context after
- Progressive disclosure: happy path → details → edge cases
- Chunking: small sections, short lists
- Tables+checklists > prose
- Spec: Business Need → Coverage → Reqs → Out of Scope. Design: ADRs → Data Model → Views → Tasks

changelog.change: Created from gentle-ai pattern: docs with low cognitive load

skills.name: oca-comment-writer
skills.title: OCA Comment Writer
skills.compact_rules:
Write warm, direct collaboration comments for OCA PRs.
- Start with actionable point. Don't recap the whole PR.
- Warm and direct: sound like a teammate, not a bot
- Keep it short: 1-3 paragraphs or bullet list
- Explain WHY when requesting changes
- Request change: observation → why matters → next action
- Approve: positive assessment → optional suggestion

changelog.change: Created from gentle-ai pattern: warm, direct collaboration comments

skills.name: odf-propose
skills.title: ODF Business Proposal
skills.compact_rules:
Phase 0: Business proposal before ASSESS.
- Question round: 3-5 business questions via question tool
- Proposal: Intent, Scope, Capabilities, Approach, Affected Areas, Risks, Rollback, Success Criteria
- NO code, NO functional spec — only the proposal
- Size budget: under 300 words
- Capabilities section is contract with ASSESS phase

changelog.change: Initial propose phase: question round + lightweight proposal before ASSESS

skills.name: odf-tdd
skills.title: ODF Strict TDD
skills.compact_rules:
Strict TDD opt-in. Tests BEFORE code (red-green-refactor).
- Every REQ-XX must have test task paired with implementation task
- Write failing test FIRST, then implement to make it pass
- VERIFY rejects code without matching tests when TDD on
- Toggle with /odf-tdd on|off

skills.name: oca-chained-pr
skills.title: OCA Chained PRs
skills.compact_rules:
Split PRs >400 lines into reviewable chained PRs.
- PR >400 lines → must split unless size:exception
- Each PR: ≤60min review, tests+docs with unit
- State boundaries: start, end, deps, follow-up, out-of-scope
- Strategies: ≤400→single, stacked to main, feature branch chain
- Dependency diagram with 📍 on current PR

changelog.change: Initial chained PR skill from gentle-ai pattern
